title: Update vendor of containers/buildah
body:
Changes since 2022-05-31:
  - add --omit-history option (buildah PR 4028)
  - add --logsplit (buildah PR 4034)

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
yes

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
podman build now supports the --omit-history option which eliminates the history field from images. 
podman build now supports --logsplit which allows you to specify a directory for build logs when using multiple platforms.  One log for each platform will be generated in the specified directory.

```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@rhatdan I don't think this is going to work on f35 due to its having go 1.16.
Yup I dropped back.  Sadly we don't have this issue on Buildah.

Looks like you need to add this back to `test/buildah-bud/apply-podman-deltas`:
```bash
skip_if_remote "omit-history not implemented in remote" \
               "build-with-omit-history-to-true should not add history"
```
(edit: or fix it so it works with remote. I tried but gave up).

While you're at it, could you edit the github description and fill in the `release-note` block? I suspect the answer is "None", but I can't be sure.
The goal of this PR is to get --omit-history to work with podman-remote, so it is actually a bug.

You can test locally with:
```console
$ env PODBIN_NAME=remote ./test/buildah-bud/run-buildah-bud-tests --filter="omit-history"
```
To test again, either add `--no-checkout` to the above or run `rm -rf test-buildah*`. (The latter is necessary if you've changed the `apply-patches` script. If you've only made changes in the podman tree, just run `make` and use the former).

@vrothberg @edsantiago I believe this now works except for a fundamental difference between buildah inspect and podman image inspect.

buildah inspect returns data about both Docker{} and OCIv1{} structure, including a History field ({{.Docker.History}} and .OCIv1.History}. Plus the inspect returns a {{.History}} field.  Podman image inspect only returns the .History field.  The buildah check is testing all 3 fields so I can not just turn the test on.  I could go back into Buildah and turn off the check for the other two fields. Or should we enhance Podman image inspect to be closer to buildah inspect?
EINSUFFICIENTBRAINPOWER (due to late hour of day). Nice catch. I will think about it in the morning.

> I could go back into Buildah and turn off the check for the other two fields. Or should we enhance Podman image inspect to be closer to buildah inspect?

I vote for changing the tests.  Changing `podman inspect` could break Docker compat.
LGTM but one more set of eyeballs please
@edsantiago @vrothberg I was mistaken, and it looks like omit-history tests are firing and passing against podman -remote, so this PR is ready to merge.
@mheon could you check the `release-note` block? On reflection it looks to me like this adds a new option (`omit-history`) and hence should include that in release notes?

@edsantiago As long as it's a Buildah change, we generally don't include it... My preference instead is to link the Buildah release notes, so that folks can look at those.

That said, `podman build` is sufficiently distinct from Buildah that I would not object if someone did include `podman build` changes in the release notes.
